fullArticle:
Read the original version in Chinese here.

Thirty years later, the UIA World Congress of Architects returns to Barcelona. Coinciding with the centenary of Antoni Gaudí’s passing, history and the present converge in this city, lending this year’s congress a unique depth of meaning.

As a triennial global architectural event organized by the International Union of Architects (UIA), this congress, themed "Becoming: Architectures for a Planet in Transition," brings together architects, scholars, and industry representatives from over 130 countries and regions. Discussions center on shared global challenges such as the climate crisis, housing, material circularity, public space, and technological innovation.
